summary:
The independent samples t-test is appropriate because it compares means of two independent groups with continuous outcome variables like height when population standard deviations are unknown and sample sizes are small to moderate. It correctly addresses whether there is a statistically significant difference in average height between 10-year-old boys and girls based on provided descriptive statistics.

We have reached an agreement among all agents regarding the selected methodological approach; therefore, no further discussion is needed.
